title: The Baptism of Our Lord
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## January 11, 2026 | Sermon: "The Baptism of Our Lord"

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The First Sunday after Epiphany (also called the Baptism of Our Lord Sunday) falls on January 11, 2026. The Revised Common Lectionary readings would include Isaiah 42:1-9 (servant passage), Psalm 29, Acts 10:34-43, and Matthew 3:13-17 (Jesus's baptism at the Jordan). This marks the transition from the Christmas/Epiphany season toward Ordinary Time, a liturgically quieter period emphasizing Christ's ministry and calling.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating the implementation of General Convention 2024 resolutions regarding expanded LGBTQ+ inclusion and liturgical innovation. The Diocese of Colorado (which includes Saint John's Cathedral in Denver) was in the midst of its regular synodal cycle, with Presiding Bishop Torey Lightcroft in the second year of her tenure. No major bishop elections or scandals were breaking in early January 2026 specifically, though the broader Anglican Communion remained fragmented over doctrinal and social policy questions.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

A winter storm system had moved through Colorado in late December 2025, leaving Denver with above-average snowpack heading into the new year. The Colorado legislature was preparing for its 2026 session (typically beginning in early February), with local media focused on water rights disputes, housing affordability crises, and proposed tax measures. Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood (where the cathedral sits) continued experiencing gentrification pressures and debates over homeless encampment policies that had intensified through 2025.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Biden administration (Biden serving through January 20, 2025) was in its final days, with transition planning to the incoming Trump administration underway. A major winter weather system affected much of the Midwest and Mountain West in early January 2026. The 2026 midterm election cycle was ramping up with early campaigning and polling beginning. Ongoing inflation concerns and Federal Reserve interest rate decisions remained prominent economic news. Major questions about healthcare, immigration policy, and climate action hung over the transition period.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine remained frozen in stalemate with diplomatic negotiations intermittently discussed but no resolution apparent. Middle East tensions persisted following years of conflict, with ongoing concerns about Iranian regional activity. The global economic situation was marked by uneven recovery with concerns about Chinese economic slowdown affecting world markets.

CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood in January 2026 reflected deep political division and uncertainty about governance direction, with many Americans bracing for significant policy shifts. This was NFL playoff season, with Denver Broncos fans either hopeful or frustrated depending on their team's trajectory into postseason play. The cultural conversation remained fractured along ideological lines regarding religion's role in public life, making a sermon on baptism's significance timely for reflecting on identity, calling, and commitment in uncertain times.
